summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates host data, npm credentials, and filesystem secrets to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell payload that fingerprints the host (hex hostname, /app working directory, presence of /app/Cargo.toml or /app/package.json) and, when matched, harvests `id` output, sorted environment variables, source files under /app (including package-lock.json and.npmrc), the contents of /app/.npmrc, /usr/etc/npmrc, and /root/.npmrc, /proc/*/cmdline, and filesystem flag/secret grep results. The collected output is base64-encoded and HTTP-PUT to `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201` (hardcoded bare IP, plaintext HTTP). The.npmrc reads expose npm auth/publish tokens, and the env dump exposes arbitrary build-environment secrets. Any installer whose environment matches the fingerprint has credentials and source contents shipped to the attacker-controlled endpoint at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
